page: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a base speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage-intensive workloads, the server includes 30000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 125019.7 and a PassMark CPU score of 87524.92, placing it in the top tier. The server demonstrates high-speed memory throughput, reaching 338.82 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 132.78 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database performance is notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 109.81 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring 30569.9. Single-core performance remains average, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1678.0.

Given its bare-metal architecture, massive local NVMe storage capacity, and high memory density, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive deployments that require direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. The combination of high-capacity local storage and strong multi-threaded processing makes it highly suitable for large-scale databases, transactional applications, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server designed for data-intensive workloads. It features 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 30000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 87524.92 and high memory bandwidth of 338.82 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by 109.81 million Redis SET operations per second. This bare-metal configuration provides direct hardware access, making it highly suitable for large-scale databases, transactional systems, and high-throughput data warehousing.

tagline: A bare-metal server offering massive local NVMe storage, high-capacity memory, and strong multi-core performance for data-intensive enterprise workloads.

30000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
